does: This paper documents OpenAI's comprehensive safety infrastructure for their Operator system, detailing concrete mitigations across multiple dimensions. The work describes model-level defenses against adversarial inputs like prompt injection and jailbreaks, product-level protections for privacy and security, external red teaming methodologies used to identify vulnerabilities, and systematic safety evaluation procedures. Rather than proposing novel techniques, it consolidates and adapts existing safety practices from prior OpenAI releases into a coherent framework tailored for an agentic system capable of taking actions in external environments.
connects: This work builds on OpenAI's established safety documentation practices exemplified by prior system cards and model cards, which have become standard artifacts for transparency in AI deployment. It extends previous work on prompt robustness and jailbreak defenses developed for language models like GPT-4, adapting these approaches for agentic systems that present novel risks through tool use and autonomous action. The red teaming methodology draws from adversarial testing practices developed across the security community and recent AI safety literature examining how language models can be misused.
enables: This document establishes a template and baseline for safety documentation in agentic AI systems, enabling future work to build more sophisticated defenses informed by disclosed vulnerabilities and limitations. By publicly detailing specific failure modes and mitigation strategies, it creates benchmarks for evaluating safety in successor systems and provides a foundation for collaborative red teaming efforts. The work points toward more rigorous, formal methods for analyzing agent safety and suggests directions for developing better evaluation metrics for agentic behavior that extend beyond language model safety.
